user_request: Draft a suit for recovery of Rs.20,00,000/- paid as advance for a business transaction which failed due to Defendant's default. Plead total failure of consideration under Section 65 of Indian Contract Act. Claim refund with interest and costs. Include cause of action paragraph with accrual date, continuing nature of cause of action, valuation, court fee, and proper verification clause. Draft suitable for filing before the District Court.

queries:
Draft a suit for recovery of Rs.20,00,000/- paid as advance for a business transaction which failed due to Defendant's default. Plead total failure of consideration under Section 65 of Indian Contract Act. Claim refund with interest and costs. Include cause of action paragraph with accrual date, continuing nature of cause of action, valuation, court fee, and proper verification clause. Draft suitable for filing before the District Court.

enrichment_log: Section 65 Indian Contract: FOUND in RAG

placeholder_dates.AGREEMENT_DATE: Date of the agreement/contract between parties
placeholder_dates.ADVANCE_PAYMENT_DATE: Date when the plaintiff made the payment/advance
placeholder_dates.PERFORMANCE_DATE: Date by which the defendant was obligated to perform
placeholder_dates.DEFAULT_DATE: Date when the defendant failed to repay/perform
placeholder_dates.NOTICE_DATE: Date when legal notice was sent
placeholder_dates.NOTICE_REPLY_DATE: Date when reply to legal notice was received
placeholder_dates.CAUSE_OF_ACTION_DATE: Date when cause of action first arose

sections.body:
SUIT FOR RECOVERY OF Rs. {{CLAIM_AMOUNT}}/- (Rupees {{CLAIM_AMOUNT_WORDS}} Only) WITH INTEREST AND COSTS
(Filed under Section 26 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908)

sections.heading: BETWEEN
sections.instruction: Draft the parties section listing plaintiff and defendant(s) with full particulars: name, age, occupation, and address. Use sui juris averment for competency. If any detail is missing, use the appropriate {{PLACEHOLDER}}.

sections.heading: JURISDICTION
sections.instruction: Draft the jurisdiction section establishing territorial and pecuniary jurisdiction. For territorial jurisdiction, state SPECIFIC facts: where the Defendant resides or carries on business, where payment was made, where the agreement was executed, or where the cause of action arose — with concrete place references. For pecuniary jurisdiction, state the claim amount and that it falls within this court's monetary limits.

sections.heading: FACTS OF THE CASE
sections.instruction: Draft the facts section as numbered paragraphs in chronological narrative form. Each paragraph must tell a story — not state conclusions. Cover: (1) How the parties came to know each other and the nature of their relationship, (2) What was agreed — the specific terms of the transaction/agreement, (3) What obligations each party undertook and the timeline for performance, (4) How payment was made — specific amount, mode, date, (5) What exactly constituted the defendant's failure — describe the specific default with detail, (6) What happened after the default — communications, demands, legal notice and response. Reference documents as 'Annexure [LABEL]'. Do NOT state legal conclusions in this section — keep it purely factual.

sections.heading: LEGAL BASIS AND GROUNDS
sections.instruction: Draft the legal basis section in paragraph form. For each statutory provision: (1) State what the provision says — the legal rule it creates, (2) Explain what factual conditions trigger its application, (3) Show how the facts of this case satisfy those conditions. If applicable, include an alternative plea (e.g., Section 73 ICA as alternative to Section 65 ICA) using 'In the alternative and without prejudice to the above...' framing. Include defensive pleading: pre-emptively neutralize likely defense arguments (e.g., no part performance was rendered, no forfeiture clause exists, no adjustment made, no counter-claim exists). Cite ONLY provisions from the verified list.

must_include.description: At least one statutory section cited
Legal consequence explained (not just section named)

sections.heading: CAUSE OF ACTION
sections.instruction: Draft the cause of action section in PARAGRAPH prose (not numbered sub-headings). Must cover: (1) ORIGIN — when and how the right to sue first arose, anchored to the default/failure date, (2) FURTHER ACCRUAL — when the cause of action further arose (e.g., failure to comply with legal notice), (3) CONTINUITY — that the cause of action is continuing because the defendant continues to wrongfully retain the money. The cause must be anchored to the failure/default date, NOT the notice date. Write in flowing paragraph form — avoid mechanical sub-headings like '(a) underlying obligation'.

sections.heading: LIMITATION
sections.instruction: Draft the limitation section stating: (1) the applicable Article of the Limitation Act, 1963 Schedule (from enrichment or use placeholder), (2) the limitation period, (3) the starting date of limitation (must be anchored to cause_of_action_date/default_date, NOT notice_date), (4) that the suit is within time. If limitation article is not available in enrichment, use {{LIMITATION_ARTICLE}} placeholder.

sections.heading: VALUATION AND COURT FEE
sections.instruction: Draft the valuation and court fee section stating: (1) suit valuation equals the claim amount, (2) court fee calculated per applicable state court fees act. Use court_fee context from web search if available, otherwise use {{COURT_FEE_AMOUNT}} placeholder.

sections.heading: INTEREST
sections.instruction: Draft the interest section with a 3-part structure: (1) Pre-suit interest — from default date to filing date with rate and justification, (2) Pendente lite interest — during pendency of suit under Section 34 CPC with rate, (3) Future interest — post-decree until realization with rate. Justify the interest rate: state why the claimed rate is appropriate (commercial transaction, wrongful retention, prevailing bank rate, contractual understanding). Cite Section 34 of CPC for pendente lite interest.

sections.heading: PRAYER
sections.instruction: Draft the prayer section with at least 6 specific sub-prayers as lettered items (a, b, c, ...). Each prayer must state exact amounts or rate placeholders — never vague. Must include: (a) decree for principal amount with Rs. figure, (b) pre-suit interest with rate and date range, (c) pendente lite interest under Section 34 CPC with rate, (d) future interest post-decree with rate until realization, (e) costs of the suit including pre-litigation costs for sending legal notice, (f) any other relief the court deems fit and proper in the facts and circumstances.

sections.heading: LIST OF DOCUMENTS RELIED UPON
sections.instruction: Generate a numbered list of documents the plaintiff relies upon, mapping to the annexure scheme. For each document: Serial No., Description, Annexure Label. Only list documents mentioned in evidence. If evidence is empty, list standard expected documents with {{DOCUMENT_DESCRIPTION}} placeholders.

sections.heading: VERIFICATION
sections.body:
I, {{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}, the Plaintiff above-named, do hereby verify that the contents of the above plaint are true and correct to my knowledge, and the contents based on legal advice are believed to be true on the basis of such advice. No part of this plaint is false and nothing material has been concealed therefrom.

Verified at {{COURT_PLACE}} on this {{FILING_DATE}}.

As per Order VI Rule 15 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908.

filled_sections.text:
SUIT FOR RECOVERY OF Rs. {{CLAIM_AMOUNT}}/- (Rupees {{CLAIM_AMOUNT_WORDS}} Only) WITH INTEREST AND COSTS
(Filed under Section 26 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908)

filled_sections.heading: BETWEEN
filled_sections.text:
Plaintiff: {{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}, aged {{PLAINTIFF_AGE}} years, {{PLAINTIFF_OCCUPATION}} by occupation, residing at {{PLAINTIFF_ADDRESS}}. The Plaintiff is sui juris and competent to sue and be sued in this action.

Defendant: {{DEFENDANT_NAME}}, aged {{DEFENDANT_AGE}} years, {{DEFENDANT_OCCUPATION}} by occupation, residing at {{DEFENDANT_ADDRESS}}.

filled_sections.heading: JURISDICTION
filled_sections.text: The Defendant, {{DEFENDANT_NAME}}, resides and carries on business at {{PLACE_NAMES}} within the territorial jurisdiction of {{COURT_NAME}} at {{COURT_PLACE}}, {{STATE}}. The advance amount under the agreement was transferred by the Plaintiff from the Plaintiff’s account with {{BANK_NAMES}} on {{ADVANCE_PAYMENT_DATE}}, a bank located within the local limits of this Court’s jurisdiction. The parties executed the agreement on {{AGREEMENT_DATE}} at {{PLACE_NAMES}}, and the cause of action first arose on {{CAUSE_OF_ACTION_DATE}} when the Defendant failed to repay/perform on {{DEFAULT_DATE}}. The Plaintiff issued a legal notice on {{NOTICE_DATE}} and received a reply on {{NOTICE_REPLY_DATE}}. This suit is filed on {{FILING_DATE}} in {{COURT_NAME}}, which has territorial and pecuniary jurisdiction to adjudicate the dispute. For pecuniary jurisdiction, the Plaintiff claims Rs. {{CLAIM_AMOUNT}} which falls within the monetary limit of Rs. {{PECUNIARY_LIMIT}} for this Court.

filled_sections.heading: FACTS OF THE CASE
filled_sections.text:
1. The Plaintiff, {{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}, and the Defendant, {{DEFENDANT_NAME}}, had prior commercial dealings and, in the course of those dealings, met to discuss a monetary arrangement. On or about {{AGREEMENT_DATE}}, they executed a written agreement evidenced by Annexure A, under which the Plaintiff agreed to provide funds to the Defendant for business purposes and to govern the repayment of the same according to the terms set out in Annexure A.

2. The terms of the agreement evidenced by Annexure A provided that an amount of Rs. {{CLAIM_AMOUNT}} would be advanced by the Plaintiff to the Defendant for the Defendant’s business needs and that the Defendant would repay the entire amount by the specified due date stated in the same document. The parties recorded the repayment obligation as a single principal sum to be paid on or before the performance date set forth in Annexure A, and the agreement further described the overall consequences of non‑payment within the same contractual framework.

3. The obligations of the parties, as described in Annexure A, were that the Plaintiff would advance the funds to the Defendant and the Defendant would repay the full principal amount by {{PERFORMANCE_DATE}}. The narrative of Annexure A also reflects that the loan was provided for a business purpose and that repayment would be in a manner contemplated by the agreement, with mechanics and proof of performance to be documented by the parties and referenced back to Annexure A.

4. On {{ADVANCE_PAYMENT_DATE}}, the Plaintiff transferred Rs. {{CLAIM_AMOUNT}} to the Defendant by bank transfer to the account details provided in Annexure A. The payment was made for the purpose specified in Annexure A, and the Defendant acknowledged receipt of the funds by notations recorded in Annexure B, which contains the payment proof. The transfer was executed from the Plaintiff’s bank account to the Defendant’s designated bank account as per the information in Annexure A.

5. By {{DEFAULT_DATE}}, the Defendant had not repaid the amount due under the agreement. No portion of the Rs. {{CLAIM_AMOUNT}} had been repaid by the Defendant by the due date stated in Annexure A, and there was no evidence of any partial payment. The Defendant also did not provide any valid justification or schedule to cure the default at that time, despite the Plaintiff’s ongoing expectations of performance under the terms of Annexure A and the total amount remaining outstanding.

6. In response to the default, the Plaintiff issued a legal notice dated {{NOTICE_DATE}} directing the Defendant to repay the advance and to comply with the repayment terms, a copy of which is placed in Annexure C. The Defendant replied to the notice on {{NOTICE_REPLY_DATE}}, and the response is recorded in Annexure D. After the notice period and the Defendant’s reply, the Plaintiff proceeded to file the suit on {{FILING_DATE}} in accordance with applicable law, with supporting documentation referenced in Annexure F as needed to establish the factual basis of the claim and the absence of performance by the Defendant. Annexures A, B, C, D, and F collectively comprise the documentary evidence supporting the facts stated above.

filled_sections.heading: LEGAL BASIS AND GROUNDS
filled_sections.text:
Section 65 of the Indian Contract Act, 1872 provides that when an agreement is discovered to be void, or when a contract becomes void, any person who has received any advantage under such agreement or contract is bound to restore it to the person from whom he received it. The legal consequence of this provision is a duty of restitution—where value or benefit conferred under a void contract has been obtained by one party, that party must restore it to the rightful owner. In the present case, the contract evidenced by Annexure A was entered into between the Plaintiff {{PLAINTIFF_NAME}} and the Defendant {{DEFENDANT_NAME}} under which the Plaintiff paid Rs. {{CLAIM_AMOUNT}} to the Defendant on {{DATE}} at {{LOCATION}}, as shown by Annexure B (payment proof). The facts establish that the Defendant did not render any goods or services in return, resulting in total failure of consideration; the Plaintiff seeks restitution of Rs. {{CLAIM_AMOUNT}} by refund to the Plaintiff, consistent with the obligation under Section 65 to restore the amount received. There is no pleaded counter-claim or set-off by the Defendant, and no forfeiture clause affecting the Plaintiff’s rights has been invoked; the Plaintiff maintains that there was no alteration or deduction that would defeat the restitution claim, as supported by the absence of any responsive documents in Annexure C to show otherwise. The Defendant may contend that some part performance or an adjustment or forfeiture exists; to pre-empt such arguments, the Plaintiff asserts that no part performance was rendered by the Defendant, no forfeiture clause binds the Plaintiff, and no statutory or contractual adjustment has been made that would reduce the restitution due. In the alternative and without prejudice to the above, if this Court finds that the contract did not become void or that Section 65 does not apply in the particular circumstances, the Plaintiff prays for restitution of the value conferred under the contract as a fallback remedy, and for such other relief as this Court deems fit, including costs and interest, to be calculated from the date of payment. This pleading remains anchored in the core statutory principle of restoring misapplied benefits and seeks to ensure full refund of the amount paid to the Defendant in the circumstances alleged, with Annexure A establishing the contract, Annexure B proving payment, and Annexure C reflecting service of any notice or response related thereto. 
---SECTION_TEXT---

validation_issues.description: Drafting-notes language: 'to be calculated' — must not appear in filed document

filled_sections.heading: CAUSE OF ACTION
filled_sections.text: The cause of action for the present suit first arose on {{DEFAULT_DATE}} when the Defendant, in breach of the contract evidenced by the agreement dated {{AGREEMENT_DATE}} executed at {{place_names}}, failed to repay the Plaintiff's advance, which the Plaintiff had on {{ADVANCE_PAYMENT_DATE}} furnished in consideration of performance by the Defendant by {{PERFORMANCE_DATE}}. The Defendant's non‑payment on {{DEFAULT_DATE}} crystallized the Plaintiff's right to sue. The cause of action further accrued thereafter as the Defendant continued to refuse to refund the amount despite the Plaintiff's demands, including the legal notice dated {{NOTICE_DATE}} demanding repayment. The Defendant neglected and refused to refund the said amount, thereby sustaining the Plaintiff's claim. The cause is a continuing one because the Defendant continues to wrongfully retain the Plaintiff's sum of {{CLAIM_AMOUNT}} without any lawful authority in {{place_names}}, and such retention persists through the date of filing and shall continue until actual realization.

filled_sections.heading: LIMITATION
filled_sections.text:
The present suit falls under the Limitation Act, 1963. The applicable Article of the Schedule to the Limitation Act, 1963 for the relief sought is Article {{LIMITATION_ARTICLE}}; in the event the enrichment does not specify a concrete article, the placeholder article governs. The limitation period prescribed by Article {{LIMITATION_ARTICLE}} runs from the date on which the cause of action first accrued, which is {{CAUSE_OF_ACTION_DATE}}, being the date when the Plaintiff and Defendant entered into the transaction dated {{AGREEMENT_DATE}} and the Defendant defaulted to repay or perform on {{DEFAULT_DATE}}. The suit was filed on {{FILING_DATE}}; on this basis, the suit is within the period of limitation applicable to the claim. The date of accrual anchors the limitation period, not the notice date, and accordingly the present action is within time under the applicable limitation article.

Limitation Act reference: Limitation Act, 1963. The present suit is within time because the accrual occurred on {{CAUSE_OF_ACTION_DATE}} (and, if applicable, the date of default {{DEFAULT_DATE}}), with filing on {{FILING_DATE}} occurring within the period prescribed by Article {{LIMITATION_ARTICLE}} of the Schedule to the Limitation Act, 1963.

validation_issues.description: Drafting-notes language: 'placeholder' — must not appear in filed document

filled_sections.heading: VALUATION AND COURT FEE
filled_sections.text: On {{FILING_DATE}}, the Plaintiff {{PLAINTIFF_NAME}} filed this suit in {{COURT_NAME}} against the Defendant {{DEFENDANT_NAME}} seeking relief in the amount of {{CLAIM_AMOUNT}}. For purposes of valuation, the suit value is declared to be the claim amount, i.e., {{CLAIM_AMOUNT}}. The court fee payable on filing this suit is calculated in accordance with the applicable State Court Fees Act, and the amount due at filing is {{COURT_FEE_AMOUNT}}.

filled_sections.heading: INTEREST
filled_sections.text:
Pre-suit interest: The Plaintiff seeks pre-suit interest on the principal amount due under the contract evidenced by the agreement dated {{AGREEMENT_DATE}} from the date of default by the Defendant on {{DEFAULT_DATE}} until the filing of the suit on {{FILING_DATE}} at the rate of {{PRE_SUIT_RATE}} per annum. The factual basis for this claim is that the Plaintiff advanced funds on {{ADVANCE_PAYMENT_DATE}} in performance of the agreement and the Defendant thereafter defaulted on {{DEFAULT_DATE}} by failing to repay the amount due, with the outstanding sum remaining unpaid up to the date of filing. The end date for this pre-suit period is {{FILING_DATE}}. The rate is justified because the transaction is commercial in nature, the Defendant has wrongfully retained funds, and the Plaintiff has been deprived of the use of its money; the contractual understanding between the parties supports compensation for the value of the use of money during this period.

Pendente lite interest: The Plaintiff seeks pendente lite interest on the same sum during the pendency of this suit from {{FILING_DATE}} to {{DECREE_DATE}} at the rate of {{PENDENTE_LITE_RATE}} per annum, under Section 34 of the CPC. The legal basis is that Section 34 CPC empowers the court to award interest on money due during the pendency of a suit, to compensate for the period over which the Defendant retains funds while litigation is ongoing. The rate is justified because the transaction is commercial in nature and the Defendant has enjoyed the benefit of wrongfully retained money; the prevailing bank lending rate for the relevant period and the contractual understanding between the parties further justify adopting a commercially reasonable rate for pendente lite interest.

Future interest: The Plaintiff seeks future interest at the rate of {{FUTURE_RATE}} per annum from the date of decree on {{DECREE_DATE}} until realization on {{REALIZATION_DATE}}. The legal basis rests on the ongoing obligation to satisfy the monetary decree and the general principle that money due under a contractual obligation bears interest until full realization. The rate is justified for several reasons: the commercial nature of the transaction, the Defendant’s continued failure to satisfy the amount due, the benefit conferred by the Plaintiff’s funds over time, and alignment with prevailing mercantile practice and contractual understanding between the parties.
---SECTION_TEXT---

filled_sections.heading: PRAYER
filled_sections.text:
PRAYER

(a) Pass a decree directing the Defendant to pay to the Plaintiff a sum of Rs. {{CLAIM_AMOUNT}}/- (Rupees {{CLAIM_AMOUNT_WORDS}} Only) being the principal amount advanced by the Plaintiff to the Defendant under the agreement dated {{AGREEMENT_DATE}} between the Plaintiff and the Defendant.

(b) Award pre-suit interest at the rate of {{PRE_SUIT_RATE}}% per annum on the said principal sum from {{ADVANCE_PAYMENT_DATE}} till {{FILING_DATE}}.

(c) Award pendente lite interest under Section 34 CPC at the rate of {{Pendente_Rate}}% per annum on the decretable amount from {{FILING_DATE}} till decree or till realisation as may be directed by the Court.

(d) Award future interest on the decretal amount at the rate of {{FUTURE_RATE}}% per annum from the date of decree till realisation.

(e) Award costs of the suit including pre-litigation costs incurred for sending the legal notice dated {{NOTICE_DATE}} and postal charges, quantified at Rs. {{COSTS_AMOUNT}}/- (Rupees {{COSTS_AMOUNT_WORDS}} Only).

(f) Grant such other relief as the Court deems fit and proper in the facts and circumstances of the case.
---SECTION_TEXT---

draft_artifacts.title: SUIT FOR RECOVERY OF Rs. {{CLAIM_AMOUNT}}/- (Rupees {{CLAIM_AMOUNT_WORDS}} Only) WITH INTEREST AND COSTS
draft_artifacts.text:
IN THE COURT OF THE {{COURT_NAME}}
AT {{COURT_PLACE}}

O.S. No. {{SUIT_NUMBER}} / {{SUIT_YEAR}}

SUIT FOR RECOVERY OF Rs. {{CLAIM_AMOUNT}}/- (Rupees {{CLAIM_AMOUNT_WORDS}} Only) WITH INTEREST AND COSTS
(Filed under Section 26 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908)


BETWEEN

Plaintiff: {{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}, aged {{PLAINTIFF_AGE}} years, {{PLAINTIFF_OCCUPATION}} by occupation, residing at {{PLAINTIFF_ADDRESS}}. The Plaintiff is sui juris and competent to sue and be sued in this action.

Defendant: {{DEFENDANT_NAME}}, aged {{DEFENDANT_AGE}} years, {{DEFENDANT_OCCUPATION}} by occupation, residing at {{DEFENDANT_ADDRESS}}.


JURISDICTION

The Defendant, {{DEFENDANT_NAME}}, resides and carries on business at {{PLACE_NAMES}} within the territorial jurisdiction of {{COURT_NAME}} at {{COURT_PLACE}}, {{STATE}}. The advance amount under the agreement was transferred by the Plaintiff from the Plaintiff’s account with {{BANK_NAMES}} on {{ADVANCE_PAYMENT_DATE}}, a bank located within the local limits of this Court’s jurisdiction. The parties executed the agreement on {{AGREEMENT_DATE}} at {{PLACE_NAMES}}, and the cause of action first arose on {{CAUSE_OF_ACTION_DATE}} when the Defendant failed to repay/perform on {{DEFAULT_DATE}}. The Plaintiff issued a legal notice on {{NOTICE_DATE}} and received a reply on {{NOTICE_REPLY_DATE}}. This suit is filed on {{FILING_DATE}} in {{COURT_NAME}}, which has territorial and pecuniary jurisdiction to adjudicate the dispute. For pecuniary jurisdiction, the Plaintiff claims Rs. {{CLAIM_AMOUNT}} which falls within the monetary limit of Rs. {{PECUNIARY_LIMIT}} for this Court.


FACTS OF THE CASE

1. The Plaintiff, {{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}, and the Defendant, {{DEFENDANT_NAME}}, had prior commercial dealings and, in the course of those dealings, met to discuss a monetary arrangement. On or about {{AGREEMENT_DATE}}, they executed a written agreement evidenced by Annexure A, under which the Plaintiff agreed to provide funds to the Defendant for business purposes and to govern the repayment of the same according to the terms set out in Annexure A.

2. The terms of the agreement evidenced by Annexure A provided that an amount of Rs. {{CLAIM_AMOUNT}} would be advanced by the Plaintiff to the Defendant for the Defendant’s business needs and that the Defendant would repay the entire amount by the specified due date stated in the same document. The parties recorded the repayment obligation as a single principal sum to be paid on or before the performance date set forth in Annexure A, and the agreement further described the overall consequences of non‑payment within the same contractual framework.

3. The obligations of the parties, as described in Annexure A, were that the Plaintiff would advance the funds to the Defendant and the Defendant would repay the full principal amount by {{PERFORMANCE_DATE}}. The narrative of Annexure A also reflects that the loan was provided for a business purpose and that repayment would be in a manner contemplated by the agreement, with mechanics and proof of performance to be documented by the parties and referenced back to Annexure A.

4. On {{ADVANCE_PAYMENT_DATE}}, the Plaintiff transferred Rs. {{CLAIM_AMOUNT}} to the Defendant by bank transfer to the account details provided in Annexure A. The payment was made for the purpose specified in Annexure A, and the Defendant acknowledged receipt of the funds by notations recorded in Annexure B, which contains the payment proof. The transfer was executed from the Plaintiff’s bank account to the Defendant’s designated bank account as per the information in Annexure A.

5. By {{DEFAULT_DATE}}, the Defendant had not repaid the amount due under the agreement. No portion of the Rs. {{CLAIM_AMOUNT}} had been repaid by the Defendant by the due date stated in Annexure A, and there was no evidence of any partial payment. The Defendant also did not provide any valid justification or schedule to cure the default at that time, despite the Plaintiff’s ongoing expectations of performance under the terms of Annexure A and the total amount remaining outstanding.

6. In response to the default, the Plaintiff issued a legal notice dated {{NOTICE_DATE}} directing the Defendant to repay the advance and to comply with the repayment terms, a copy of which is placed in Annexure C. The Defendant replied to the notice on {{NOTICE_REPLY_DATE}}, and the response is recorded in Annexure D. After the notice period and the Defendant’s reply, the Plaintiff proceeded to file the suit on {{FILING_DATE}} in accordance with applicable law, with supporting documentation referenced in Annexure F as needed to establish the factual basis of the claim and the absence of performance by the Defendant. Annexures A, B, C, D, and F collectively comprise the documentary evidence supporting the facts stated above.


LEGAL BASIS AND GROUNDS

Section 65 of the Indian Contract Act, 1872 provides that when an agreement is discovered to be void, or when a contract becomes void, any person who has received any advantage under such agreement or contract is bound to restore it to the person from whom he received it. The legal consequence of this provision is a duty of restitution—where value or benefit conferred under a void contract has been obtained by one party, that party must restore it to the rightful owner. In the present case, the contract evidenced by Annexure A was entered into between the Plaintiff {{PLAINTIFF_NAME}} and the Defendant {{DEFENDANT_NAME}} under which the Plaintiff paid Rs. {{CLAIM_AMOUNT}} to the Defendant on {{DATE}} at {{LOCATION}}, as shown by Annexure B (payment proof). The facts establish that the Defendant did not render any goods or services in return, resulting in total failure of consideration; the Plaintiff seeks restitution of Rs. {{CLAIM_AMOUNT}} by refund to the Plaintiff, consistent with the obligation under Section 65 to restore the amount received. There is no pleaded counter-claim or set-off by the Defendant, and no forfeiture clause affecting the Plaintiff’s rights has been invoked; the Plaintiff maintains that there was no alteration or deduction that would defeat the restitution claim, as supported by the absence of any responsive documents in Annexure C to show otherwise. The Defendant may contend that some part performance or an adjustment or forfeiture exists; to pre-empt such arguments, the Plaintiff asserts that no part performance was rendered by the Defendant, no forfeiture clause binds the Plaintiff, and no statutory or contractual adjustment has been made that would reduce the restitution due. In the alternative and without prejudice to the above, if this Court finds that the contract did not become void or that Section 65 does not apply in the particular circumstances, the Plaintiff prays for restitution of the value conferred under the contract as a fallback remedy, and for such other relief as this Court deems fit, including costs and interest, to be calculated from the date of payment. This pleading remains anchored in the core statutory principle of restoring misapplied benefits and seeks to ensure full refund of the amount paid to the Defendant in the circumstances alleged, with Annexure A establishing the contract, Annexure B proving payment, and Annexure C reflecting service of any notice or response related thereto. 

---SECTION_TEXT---


CAUSE OF ACTION

The cause of action for the present suit first arose on {{DEFAULT_DATE}} when the Defendant, in breach of the contract evidenced by the agreement dated {{AGREEMENT_DATE}} executed at {{place_names}}, failed to repay the Plaintiff's advance, which the Plaintiff had on {{ADVANCE_PAYMENT_DATE}} furnished in consideration of performance by the Defendant by {{PERFORMANCE_DATE}}. The Defendant's non‑payment on {{DEFAULT_DATE}} crystallized the Plaintiff's right to sue. The cause of action further accrued thereafter as the Defendant continued to refuse to refund the amount despite the Plaintiff's demands, including the legal notice dated {{NOTICE_DATE}} demanding repayment. The Defendant neglected and refused to refund the said amount, thereby sustaining the Plaintiff's claim. The cause is a continuing one because the Defendant continues to wrongfully retain the Plaintiff's sum of {{CLAIM_AMOUNT}} without any lawful authority in {{place_names}}, and such retention persists through the date of filing and shall continue until actual realization.


LIMITATION

The present suit falls under the Limitation Act, 1963. The applicable Article of the Schedule to the Limitation Act, 1963 for the relief sought is Article {{LIMITATION_ARTICLE}}; in the event the enrichment does not specify a concrete article, the placeholder article governs. The limitation period prescribed by Article {{LIMITATION_ARTICLE}} runs from the date on which the cause of action first accrued, which is {{CAUSE_OF_ACTION_DATE}}, being the date when the Plaintiff and Defendant entered into the transaction dated {{AGREEMENT_DATE}} and the Defendant defaulted to repay or perform on {{DEFAULT_DATE}}. The suit was filed on {{FILING_DATE}}; on this basis, the suit is within the period of limitation applicable to the claim. The date of accrual anchors the limitation period, not the notice date, and accordingly the present action is within time under the applicable limitation article.

Limitation Act reference: Limitation Act, 1963. The present suit is within time because the accrual occurred on {{CAUSE_OF_ACTION_DATE}} (and, if applicable, the date of default {{DEFAULT_DATE}}), with filing on {{FILING_DATE}} occurring within the period prescribed by Article {{LIMITATION_ARTICLE}} of the Schedule to the Limitation Act, 1963.


VALUATION AND COURT FEE

On {{FILING_DATE}}, the Plaintiff {{PLAINTIFF_NAME}} filed this suit in {{COURT_NAME}} against the Defendant {{DEFENDANT_NAME}} seeking relief in the amount of {{CLAIM_AMOUNT}}. For purposes of valuation, the suit value is declared to be the claim amount, i.e., {{CLAIM_AMOUNT}}. The court fee payable on filing this suit is calculated in accordance with the applicable State Court Fees Act, and the amount due at filing is {{COURT_FEE_AMOUNT}}.


INTEREST

Pre-suit interest: The Plaintiff seeks pre-suit interest on the principal amount due under the contract evidenced by the agreement dated {{AGREEMENT_DATE}} from the date of default by the Defendant on {{DEFAULT_DATE}} until the filing of the suit on {{FILING_DATE}} at the rate of {{PRE_SUIT_RATE}} per annum. The factual basis for this claim is that the Plaintiff advanced funds on {{ADVANCE_PAYMENT_DATE}} in performance of the agreement and the Defendant thereafter defaulted on {{DEFAULT_DATE}} by failing to repay the amount due, with the outstanding sum remaining unpaid up to the date of filing. The end date for this pre-suit period is {{FILING_DATE}}. The rate is justified because the transaction is commercial in nature, the Defendant has wrongfully retained funds, and the Plaintiff has been deprived of the use of its money; the contractual understanding between the parties supports compensation for the value of the use of money during this period.

Pendente lite interest: The Plaintiff seeks pendente lite interest on the same sum during the pendency of this suit from {{FILING_DATE}} to {{DECREE_DATE}} at the rate of {{PENDENTE_LITE_RATE}} per annum, under Section 34 of the CPC. The legal basis is that Section 34 CPC empowers the court to award interest on money due during the pendency of a suit, to compensate for the period over which the Defendant retains funds while litigation is ongoing. The rate is justified because the transaction is commercial in nature and the Defendant has enjoyed the benefit of wrongfully retained money; the prevailing bank lending rate for the relevant period and the contractual understanding between the parties further justify adopting a commercially reasonable rate for pendente lite interest.

Future interest: The Plaintiff seeks future interest at the rate of {{FUTURE_RATE}} per annum from the date of decree on {{DECREE_DATE}} until realization on {{REALIZATION_DATE}}. The legal basis rests on the ongoing obligation to satisfy the monetary decree and the general principle that money due under a contractual obligation bears interest until full realization. The rate is justified for several reasons: the commercial nature of the transaction, the Defendant’s continued failure to satisfy the amount due, the benefit conferred by the Plaintiff’s funds over time, and alignment with prevailing mercantile practice and contractual understanding between the parties.

---SECTION_TEXT---


PRAYER

PRAYER

(a) Pass a decree directing the Defendant to pay to the Plaintiff a sum of Rs. {{CLAIM_AMOUNT}}/- (Rupees {{CLAIM_AMOUNT_WORDS}} Only) being the principal amount advanced by the Plaintiff to the Defendant under the agreement dated {{AGREEMENT_DATE}} between the Plaintiff and the Defendant.

(b) Award pre-suit interest at the rate of {{PRE_SUIT_RATE}}% per annum on the said principal sum from {{ADVANCE_PAYMENT_DATE}} till {{FILING_DATE}}.

(c) Award pendente lite interest under Section 34 CPC at the rate of {{Pendente_Rate}}% per annum on the decretable amount from {{FILING_DATE}} till decree or till realisation as may be directed by the Court.

(d) Award future interest on the decretal amount at the rate of {{FUTURE_RATE}}% per annum from the date of decree till realisation.

(e) Award costs of the suit including pre-litigation costs incurred for sending the legal notice dated {{NOTICE_DATE}} and postal charges, quantified at Rs. {{COSTS_AMOUNT}}/- (Rupees {{COSTS_AMOUNT_WORDS}} Only).

(f) Grant such other relief as the Court deems fit and proper in the facts and circumstances of the case.

---SECTION_TEXT---


LIST OF DOCUMENTS RELIED UPON

7. Serial No. 1; Description: {{DOCUMENT_DESCRIPTION}}; Annexure Label: Annexure A
8. Serial No. 2; Description: {{DOCUMENT_DESCRIPTION}}; Annexure Label: Annexure B
9. Serial No. 3; Description: {{DOCUMENT_DESCRIPTION}}; Annexure Label: Annexure C
10. Serial No. 4; Description: {{DOCUMENT_DESCRIPTION}}; Annexure Label: Annexure D
11. Serial No. 5; Description: {{DOCUMENT_DESCRIPTION}}; Annexure Label: Annexure E
12. Serial No. 6; Description: {{DOCUMENT_DESCRIPTION}}; Annexure Label: Annexure F


VERIFICATION

I, {{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}, the Plaintiff above-named, do hereby verify that the contents of the above plaint are true and correct to my knowledge, and the contents based on legal advice are believed to be true on the basis of such advice. No part of this plaint is false and nothing material has been concealed therefrom.

Verified at {{COURT_PLACE}} on this {{FILING_DATE}}.

As per Order VI Rule 15 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908.

Through:
{{ADVOCATE_NAME}}
Advocate
Enrollment No.: {{ADVOCATE_ENROLLMENT}}
Place: {{COURT_PLACE}}
Date: {{FILING_DATE}}

draft_artifacts.title: SUIT FOR RECOVERY OF Rs. 20,00,000/- (Rupees Twenty Lakhs Only) WITH INTEREST AND COSTS
draft_artifacts.text:
IN THE COURT OF THE {{COURT_NAME}}
AT {{COURT_PLACE}}

O.S. No. {{SUIT_NUMBER}} / {{SUIT_YEAR}}

SUIT FOR RECOVERY OF Rs. 20,00,000/- (Rupees Twenty Lakhs Only) WITH INTEREST AND COSTS
(Filed under Section 26 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908)

BETWEEN

Plaintiff: {{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}, aged {{PLAINTIFF_AGE}} years, {{PLAINTIFF_OCCUPATION}} by occupation, residing at {{PLAINTIFF_ADDRESS}}. The Plaintiff is sui juris and competent to sue and be sued.

Defendant: {{DEFENDANT_NAME}}, aged {{DEFENDANT_AGE}} years, {{DEFENDANT_OCCUPATION}} by occupation, residing at {{DEFENDANT_ADDRESS}}.

JURISDICTION

This Hon’ble Court has territorial jurisdiction as the Defendant resides and carries on business at {{PLACE_NAMES}} within the local limits of this Court. The advance amount of Rs. 20,00,000/- was transferred by the Plaintiff from the Plaintiff’s bank account maintained with {{BANK_NAMES}} on {{ADVANCE_PAYMENT_DATE}} and credited to the Defendant’s bank account as per the transaction understanding between the parties. The transaction discussions/understanding were finalised at {{PLACE_NAMES}} on or about {{AGREEMENT_DATE}}. This Hon’ble Court has pecuniary jurisdiction as the suit valuation is Rs. 20,00,000/-, which falls within the monetary competence of this Court.

FACTS OF THE CASE

1. The Plaintiff and the Defendant entered into discussions for a business transaction/arrangement, namely: {{BUSINESS_TRANSACTION_DESCRIPTION}} (“the Transaction”). The Transaction was to be completed on the Defendant fulfilling certain agreed obligations/steps by {{PERFORMANCE_DATE}}, including: {{DEFENDANT_OBLIGATIONS_SUMMARY}}.

2. Pursuant to the Transaction, the parties recorded the essential terms in a written document/term sheet/confirmation dated {{AGREEMENT_DATE}} (“the Agreement”), which is produced as Annexure A. Under the Agreement, the Plaintiff agreed to pay an advance of Rs. 20,00,000/- to the Defendant towards the Transaction, and the Defendant agreed to complete the Transaction and apply the advance strictly for the Transaction and to refund the advance in case the Transaction could not be completed due to the Defendant’s default and there was a total failure of consideration.

3. In performance of the Plaintiff’s part of the Agreement, the Plaintiff transferred an amount of Rs. 20,00,000/- to the Defendant on {{ADVANCE_PAYMENT_DATE}} by bank transfer to the Defendant’s account details communicated by the Defendant. The bank transfer proof/statement is filed as Annexure B.

4. The Plaintiff states that the advance was paid specifically and only for the purpose of the Transaction. The Plaintiff did not receive any goods, services, transfer of interest, assets, shares, licences, rights, or any other agreed benefit in return for the said advance.

5. The Defendant failed to perform the Defendant’s obligations under the Transaction by {{DEFAULT_DATE}} / {{PERFORMANCE_DATE}} and thereby defaulted. Due to the Defendant’s default, the Transaction could not be completed and the Agreement became void and was discovered to be void in the sense that the contemplated Transaction failed and could not be lawfully or practically completed on the agreed terms.

6. The Plaintiff repeatedly demanded refund of the advance. The Plaintiff also caused a legal notice dated {{NOTICE_DATE}} to be issued to the Defendant calling upon the Defendant to refund Rs. 20,00,000/- within the stipulated time, which notice is filed as Annexure C. The Defendant sent a reply dated {{NOTICE_REPLY_DATE}} (if any), filed as Annexure D. The Defendant has failed and neglected to refund the advance till date. The parties’ further correspondence/reminders (if any) are collectively filed as Annexure F.

LEGAL BASIS AND GROUNDS

7. Section 65 of the Indian Contract Act, 1872 provides that when an agreement is discovered to be void, or when a contract becomes void, any person who has received any advantage under such agreement or contract is bound to restore it to the person from whom he received it.

8. In the present case, the Defendant received an advantage of Rs. 20,00,000/- from the Plaintiff as an advance towards the Transaction, as evidenced by Annexure A (Agreement/record of terms) and Annexure B (payment proof). The Transaction failed due to the Defendant’s default and the Agreement consequently became void and was discovered to be void, resulting in total failure of consideration, as the Plaintiff received no agreed benefit whatsoever.

9. Accordingly, the Defendant is bound to restore/refund the said amount of Rs. 20,00,000/- to the Plaintiff under Section 65 of the Indian Contract Act, 1872. The Defendant has no contractual or lawful basis to retain the Plaintiff’s money. The Plaintiff further states that no forfeiture clause has been validly invoked against the Plaintiff, and the Defendant has rendered no part performance that could justify retention or adjustment of the advance.

CAUSE OF ACTION

10. The cause of action first arose on {{DEFAULT_DATE}} / {{PERFORMANCE_DATE}} when the Defendant failed to perform the Transaction and failed to complete the agreed obligations, and the Plaintiff’s right to seek refund/restitution of the advance crystallised. The cause of action further accrued on each occasion thereafter when the Plaintiff demanded refund (including by legal notice dated {{NOTICE_DATE}}) and the Defendant failed/refused to refund. The cause of action is continuing as the Defendant continues to wrongfully retain the Plaintiff’s sum of Rs. 20,00,000/- without lawful authority, and such wrongful retention continues up to the date of filing and will continue until actual realization.

LIMITATION

11. The present suit is filed within the period of limitation prescribed under the Limitation Act, 1963 for a suit seeking refund/restitution of money paid as advance where consideration has totally failed. Without prejudice, the Plaintiff states that the applicable Article is {{LIMITATION_ARTICLE}} of the Schedule to the Limitation Act, 1963, and the limitation is computed from {{LIMITATION_START_DATE}} (being the date on which the right to sue first accrued as pleaded above). The suit filed on {{FILING_DATE}} is within limitation.

VALUATION AND COURT FEE

12. For the purposes of court fee and jurisdiction, the suit is valued at Rs. 20,00,000/- (Rupees Twenty Lakhs Only), being the amount sought to be recovered. The court fee payable is as per the applicable State Court Fees Act and is paid/liable to be paid as {{COURT_FEE_AMOUNT}}.

INTEREST

13. Pre-suit interest: The Plaintiff seeks pre-suit interest on the principal sum of Rs. 20,00,000/- from {{DEFAULT_DATE}} till {{FILING_DATE}} at the rate of {{PRE_SUIT_RATE}}% per annum, as the Transaction was commercial in nature and the Defendant has wrongfully retained the Plaintiff’s money, depriving the Plaintiff of its use.

14. Pendente lite interest: The Plaintiff seeks pendente lite interest under Section 34 CPC at the rate of {{PENDENTE_LITE_RATE}}% per annum from {{FILING_DATE}} till the date of decree, or such other period as this Hon’ble Court may direct.

15. Future interest: The Plaintiff seeks future interest at the rate of {{FUTURE_RATE}}% per annum from the date of decree till realization.

PRAYER

In view of the facts stated above, the Plaintiff most respectfully prays that this Hon’ble Court be pleased to:

(a) Pass a decree directing the Defendant to pay to the Plaintiff a sum of Rs. 20,00,000/- (Rupees Twenty Lakhs Only) being the advance paid by the Plaintiff to the Defendant for the Transaction which failed due to the Defendant’s default;

(b) Award pre-suit interest at the rate of {{PRE_SUIT_RATE}}% per annum on Rs. 20,00,000/- from {{DEFAULT_DATE}} till {{FILING_DATE}};

(c) Award pendente lite interest under Section 34 CPC at the rate of {{PENDENTE_LITE_RATE}}% per annum on the decretal amount from {{FILING_DATE}} till the date of decree (or till realisation as may be directed by this Hon’ble Court);

(d) Award future interest at the rate of {{FUTURE_RATE}}% per annum on the decretal amount from the date of decree till realisation;

(e) Award costs of the suit including the costs incurred towards issuance of legal notice dated {{NOTICE_DATE}} and incidental charges; and

(f) Grant such other relief(s) as this Hon’ble Court may deem fit and proper in the facts and circumstances of the case.

LIST OF DOCUMENTS RELIED UPON

1. Annexure A: {{ANNEXURE_A_DESCRIPTION}} (Agreement/term sheet/record of terms dated {{AGREEMENT_DATE}})
2. Annexure B: {{ANNEXURE_B_DESCRIPTION}} (Bank transfer proof/statement evidencing payment dated {{ADVANCE_PAYMENT_DATE}})
3. Annexure C: {{ANNEXURE_C_DESCRIPTION}} (Legal notice dated {{NOTICE_DATE}})
4. Annexure D: {{ANNEXURE_D_DESCRIPTION}} (Reply, if any, dated {{NOTICE_REPLY_DATE}})
5. Annexure F: {{ANNEXURE_F_DESCRIPTION}} (Correspondence/reminders, if any)

VERIFICATION

I, {{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}, the Plaintiff above-named, do hereby verify that the contents of the above plaint are true and correct to my knowledge, and the contents based on legal advice are believed to be true on the basis of such advice. No part of this plaint is false and nothing material has been concealed therefrom.

Verified at {{COURT_PLACE}} on this {{FILING_DATE}}.

As per Order VI Rule 15 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908.

Through:
{{ADVOCATE_NAME}}
Advocate
Enrollment No.: {{ADVOCATE_ENROLLMENT}}
Place: {{COURT_PLACE}}
Date: {{FILING_DATE}}

placeholders_used.reason: To be assigned by registry/unknown at drafting stage
To be assigned by registry/unknown at drafting stage
Notice date not provided (if notice issued)
Reply date not provided (if reply received)
Jurisdiction/cause-specific limitation article not confirmed in provided context
To be set based on pleaded accrual/failure date

citations_used: Order VI Rule 15, Code of Civil Procedure, 1908

blocking_issues.issue: Draft contains drafting-note marker text "---SECTION_TEXT---" which is not pleading language and should not appear in a filed plaint.
blocking_issues.fix: Delete all occurrences of "---SECTION_TEXT---" and ensure section breaks are represented only through proper headings/spacing.
blocking_issues.location: After "LEGAL BASIS AND GROUNDS" section; after "INTEREST" section; after "PRAYER" section

blocking_issues.issue: Section 65 Indian Contract Act is pleaded, but the draft’s factual matrix reads like an ordinary repayable loan/breach claim and does not plead the statutory trigger (agreement discovered to be void / contract becomes void) or facts showing how the underlying business transaction became void, leading to restitution for total failure of consideration.
blocking_issues.fix: Rewrite the facts and legal basis to (i) clearly characterise the payment as an advance for a business transaction, not a loan; (ii) plead that due to Defendant’s default the contemplated transaction could not be completed and the agreement became void/was discovered to be void; and (iii) plead total failure of consideration and restitution under Section 65 with clear linkage between facts and the statutory trigger.
blocking_issues.location: FACTS OF THE CASE paragraphs 1–6; LEGAL BASIS AND GROUNDS section

blocking_issues.issue: Limitation section contains speculative/placeholder-governing language and internally inconsistent accrual anchoring (mixing agreement date and default date), which is not suitable for filing.
blocking_issues.fix: Replace the limitation paragraphs with a clean pleading that (i) identifies the accrual date from pleaded facts (default/failure date), (ii) pleads that the suit is within limitation under the applicable Article (kept as a placeholder if not confirmed), and (iii) removes speculative language like "placeholder article governs".

blocking_issues.issue: Document reference label mismatch: Annexure E is listed but not referenced in the body; numbering in list of documents starts from 7, creating an internal consistency defect.
blocking_issues.fix: Either reference Annexure E in the body or delete Annexure E from the List of Documents; renumber the list from Serial No. 1 onward and ensure every annexure referenced in the body appears in the list (and vice versa).
blocking_issues.location: FACTS OF THE CASE paragraph 6; LIST OF DOCUMENTS RELIED UPON

blocking_issues.issue: Known user-provided claim amount (Rs. 20,00,000/-) is left as a template placeholder throughout, affecting valuation, prayer, and suit description and making the draft not filing-ready as-is.
blocking_issues.fix: Replace {{CLAIM_AMOUNT}} and {{CLAIM_AMOUNT_WORDS}} with '20,00,000' and 'Twenty Lakhs' (and ensure consistent usage across title, body, valuation, and prayer).
blocking_issues.location: Title; Jurisdiction; Facts; Legal Basis; Cause of Action; Valuation and Court Fee; Prayer

blocking_issues.issue: Inconsistent placeholder key for pendente lite interest rate in Prayer (uses {{Pendente_Rate}} instead of {{PENDENTE_LITE_RATE}} used elsewhere), which can lead to an incorrect/blank rate in the final filed version.
blocking_issues.fix: Use a single consistent placeholder key for pendente lite interest rate throughout (prefer {{PENDENTE_LITE_RATE}}).

non_blocking_issues.issue: Legal analysis is mixed into the Jurisdiction paragraph (cause of action narration and procedural steps are included).
non_blocking_issues.fix: Keep Jurisdiction focused on territorial/pecuniary facts; shift cause-of-action narrative to the Cause of Action section.

non_blocking_issues.issue: Prayer heading is duplicated ("PRAYER" appears twice).
non_blocking_issues.fix: Remove one of the duplicate headings.

non_blocking_issues.issue: Interest prayers are mostly specific, but clause (c) could mirror the phrasing used in the Interest section ("from filing till decree") and keep capitalization consistent.
non_blocking_issues.fix: Standardize phrasing and capitalization across interest clauses and sections.

non_blocking_issues.issue: Cause of action paragraph is generally adequate, but could be tightened by referencing the precise event by which the transaction became void/discovered void (in addition to default).
non_blocking_issues.fix: Add one sentence stating when/how the transaction was terminated/failed and thereby became void/discovered void, consistent with Section 65 pleading.

unsupported_statements.statement: "On or about {{AGREEMENT_DATE}}, they executed a written agreement evidenced by Annexure A"
unsupported_statements.reason: USER_REQUEST does not state that there is a written agreement/term sheet; existence and form of Annexure A is not supported by provided context.

unsupported_statements.statement: "The Defendant acknowledged receipt of the funds by notations recorded in Annexure B"
unsupported_statements.reason: USER_REQUEST does not mention any acknowledgment document/notations; Annexure B content is not supported by provided context.

unsupported_statements.statement: "The Plaintiff issued a legal notice dated {{NOTICE_DATE}}... The Defendant replied... recorded in Annexure D"
unsupported_statements.reason: USER_REQUEST does not mention a legal notice or reply; these procedural facts/documents are not supported by provided context.
unsupported_statements.location: FACTS OF THE CASE para 6; JURISDICTION paragraph

unsupported_statements.statement: "No portion of the Rs. {{CLAIM_AMOUNT}} had been repaid..."
unsupported_statements.reason: USER_REQUEST states the transaction failed due to Defendant’s default but does not specify whether any partial refund/adjustment was made; the 'no portion repaid' assertion is not supported by provided context.

final_artifacts.title: SUIT FOR RECOVERY OF Rs. 20,00,000/- (Rupees Twenty Lakhs Only) WITH INTEREST AND COSTS
final_artifacts.text:
IN THE COURT OF THE {{COURT_NAME}}
AT {{COURT_PLACE}}

O.S. No. {{SUIT_NUMBER}} / {{SUIT_YEAR}}

SUIT FOR RECOVERY OF Rs. 20,00,000/- (Rupees Twenty Lakhs Only) WITH INTEREST AND COSTS
(Filed under Section 26 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908)

BETWEEN

Plaintiff: {{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}, aged {{PLAINTIFF_AGE}} years, {{PLAINTIFF_OCCUPATION}} by occupation, residing at {{PLAINTIFF_ADDRESS}}. The Plaintiff is sui juris and competent to sue and be sued.

Defendant: {{DEFENDANT_NAME}}, aged {{DEFENDANT_AGE}} years, {{DEFENDANT_OCCUPATION}} by occupation, residing at {{DEFENDANT_ADDRESS}}.

JURISDICTION

This Hon’ble Court has territorial jurisdiction as the Defendant resides and carries on business at {{PLACE_NAMES}} within the local limits of this Court. The advance amount of Rs. 20,00,000/- was transferred by the Plaintiff from the Plaintiff’s bank account maintained with {{BANK_NAMES}} on {{ADVANCE_PAYMENT_DATE}} and credited to the Defendant’s bank account as per the transaction understanding between the parties. The transaction discussions/understanding were finalised at {{PLACE_NAMES}} on or about {{AGREEMENT_DATE}}. This Hon’ble Court has pecuniary jurisdiction as the suit valuation is Rs. 20,00,000/-, which falls within the monetary competence of this Court.

FACTS OF THE CASE

1. The Plaintiff and the Defendant entered into discussions for a business transaction/arrangement, namely: {{BUSINESS_TRANSACTION_DESCRIPTION}} (“the Transaction”). The Transaction was to be completed on the Defendant fulfilling certain agreed obligations/steps by {{PERFORMANCE_DATE}}, including: {{DEFENDANT_OBLIGATIONS_SUMMARY}}.

2. Pursuant to the Transaction, the parties recorded the essential terms in a written document/term sheet/confirmation dated {{AGREEMENT_DATE}} (“the Agreement”), which is produced as Annexure A. Under the Agreement, the Plaintiff agreed to pay an advance of Rs. 20,00,000/- to the Defendant towards the Transaction, and the Defendant agreed to complete the Transaction and/or apply the advance strictly for the Transaction and to refund the advance in case the Transaction could not be completed due to the Defendant’s default and there was a total failure of consideration.

3. In performance of the Plaintiff’s part of the Agreement, the Plaintiff transferred an amount of Rs. 20,00,000/- to the Defendant on {{ADVANCE_PAYMENT_DATE}} by bank transfer to the Defendant’s account details communicated by the Defendant. The bank transfer proof/statement is filed as Annexure B.

4. The Plaintiff states that the advance was paid specifically and only for the purpose of the Transaction. The Plaintiff did not receive any goods, services, transfer of interest, assets, shares, licences, rights, or any other agreed benefit in return for the said advance.

5. The Defendant failed to perform the Defendant’s obligations under the Transaction by {{DEFAULT_DATE}} / {{PERFORMANCE_DATE}} and thereby defaulted. Due to the Defendant’s default, the Transaction could not be completed and the Agreement became void and/or was discovered to be void in the sense that the contemplated Transaction failed and could not be lawfully or practically completed on the agreed terms.

6. The Plaintiff repeatedly demanded refund of the advance. The Plaintiff also caused a legal notice dated {{NOTICE_DATE}} to be issued to the Defendant calling upon the Defendant to refund Rs. 20,00,000/- within the stipulated time, which notice is filed as Annexure C. The Defendant sent a reply dated {{NOTICE_REPLY_DATE}} (if any), filed as Annexure D. The Defendant has failed and neglected to refund the advance till date. The parties’ further correspondence/reminders (if any) are collectively filed as Annexure F.

LEGAL BASIS AND GROUNDS

7. Section 65 of the Indian Contract Act, 1872 provides that when an agreement is discovered to be void, or when a contract becomes void, any person who has received any advantage under such agreement or contract is bound to restore it to the person from whom he received it.

8. In the present case, the Defendant received an advantage of Rs. 20,00,000/- from the Plaintiff as an advance towards the Transaction, as evidenced by Annexure A (Agreement/record of terms) and Annexure B (payment proof). The Transaction failed due to the Defendant’s default and the Agreement consequently became void and/or was discovered to be void, resulting in total failure of consideration, as the Plaintiff received no agreed benefit whatsoever.

9. Accordingly, the Defendant is bound to restore/refund the said amount of Rs. 20,00,000/- to the Plaintiff under Section 65 of the Indian Contract Act, 1872. The Defendant has no contractual or lawful basis to retain the Plaintiff’s money. The Plaintiff further states that no forfeiture clause has been validly invoked against the Plaintiff, and the Defendant has rendered no part performance that could justify retention or adjustment of the advance.

CAUSE OF ACTION

10. The cause of action first arose on {{DEFAULT_DATE}} / {{PERFORMANCE_DATE}} when the Defendant failed to perform the Transaction and failed to complete the agreed obligations, and the Plaintiff’s right to seek refund/restitution of the advance crystallised. The cause of action further accrued on each occasion thereafter when the Plaintiff demanded refund (including by legal notice dated {{NOTICE_DATE}}) and the Defendant failed/refused to refund. The cause of action is continuing as the Defendant continues to wrongfully retain the Plaintiff’s sum of Rs. 20,00,000/- without lawful authority, and such wrongful retention continues up to the date of filing and will continue until actual realization.

LIMITATION

11. The present suit is filed within the period of limitation prescribed under the Limitation Act, 1963 for a suit seeking refund/restitution of money paid as advance where consideration has totally failed. Without prejudice, the Plaintiff states that the applicable Article is {{LIMITATION_ARTICLE}} of the Schedule to the Limitation Act, 1963, and the limitation is computed from {{LIMITATION_START_DATE}} (being the date on which the right to sue first accrued as pleaded above). The suit filed on {{FILING_DATE}} is within limitation.

VALUATION AND COURT FEE

12. For the purposes of court fee and jurisdiction, the suit is valued at Rs. 20,00,000/- (Rupees Twenty Lakhs Only), being the amount sought to be recovered. The court fee payable is as per the applicable State Court Fees Act and is paid/liable to be paid as {{COURT_FEE_AMOUNT}}.

INTEREST

13. Pre-suit interest: The Plaintiff seeks pre-suit interest on the principal sum of Rs. 20,00,000/- from {{DEFAULT_DATE}} till {{FILING_DATE}} at the rate of {{PRE_SUIT_RATE}}% per annum, as the Transaction was commercial in nature and the Defendant has wrongfully retained the Plaintiff’s money, depriving the Plaintiff of its use.

14. Pendente lite interest: The Plaintiff seeks pendente lite interest under Section 34 CPC at the rate of {{PENDENTE_LITE_RATE}}% per annum from {{FILING_DATE}} till the date of decree, or such other period as this Hon’ble Court may direct.

15. Future interest: The Plaintiff seeks future interest at the rate of {{FUTURE_RATE}}% per annum from the date of decree till realization.

PRAYER

In view of the facts stated above, the Plaintiff most respectfully prays that this Hon’ble Court be pleased to:

(a) Pass a decree directing the Defendant to pay to the Plaintiff a sum of Rs. 20,00,000/- (Rupees Twenty Lakhs Only) being the advance paid by the Plaintiff to the Defendant for the Transaction which failed due to the Defendant’s default;

(b) Award pre-suit interest at the rate of {{PRE_SUIT_RATE}}% per annum on Rs. 20,00,000/- from {{DEFAULT_DATE}} till {{FILING_DATE}};

(c) Award pendente lite interest under Section 34 CPC at the rate of {{PENDENTE_LITE_RATE}}% per annum on the decretal amount from {{FILING_DATE}} till the date of decree (or till realisation as may be directed by this Hon’ble Court);

(d) Award future interest at the rate of {{FUTURE_RATE}}% per annum on the decretal amount from the date of decree till realisation;

(e) Award costs of the suit including the costs incurred towards issuance of legal notice dated {{NOTICE_DATE}} and incidental charges; and

(f) Grant such other relief(s) as this Hon’ble Court may deem fit and proper in the facts and circumstances of the case.

LIST OF DOCUMENTS RELIED UPON

1. Annexure A: {{ANNEXURE_A_DESCRIPTION}} (Agreement/term sheet/record of terms dated {{AGREEMENT_DATE}})
2. Annexure B: {{ANNEXURE_B_DESCRIPTION}} (Bank transfer proof/statement evidencing payment dated {{ADVANCE_PAYMENT_DATE}})
3. Annexure C: {{ANNEXURE_C_DESCRIPTION}} (Legal notice dated {{NOTICE_DATE}})
4. Annexure D: {{ANNEXURE_D_DESCRIPTION}} (Reply, if any, dated {{NOTICE_REPLY_DATE}})
5. Annexure F: {{ANNEXURE_F_DESCRIPTION}} (Correspondence/reminders, if any)

VERIFICATION

I, {{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}, the Plaintiff above-named, do hereby verify that the contents of the above plaint are true and correct to my knowledge, and the contents based on legal advice are believed to be true on the basis of such advice. No part of this plaint is false and nothing material has been concealed therefrom.

Verified at {{COURT_PLACE}} on this {{FILING_DATE}}.

As per Order VI Rule 15 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908.

Through:
{{ADVOCATE_NAME}}
Advocate

Place: {{COURT_PLACE}}
Date: {{FILING_DATE}}
